stemHtml: A pilot is taken in for a meeting with their manager due to delays to departure across the airline fleet. The manager tells the pilot to continue being safety focused but that frost on the wings is insignificant and doesn't need de-icing. The next flight, the pilot spots frost on top of the wing. What should the pilot do?

options.a: Call the manager and ask for their opinion.
options.b: Take-off as the pilot has been told not to worry about it.
options.c: Request de-icing as a small layer of frost could considerably affect the lift of the wings.
options.d: Take-off only if the fuselage is clear of contamination.
